summary:
Bacterial genetics research indicates that phages facilitate the transfer of genes via transduction, specifically involving Corynebacterium diphtheriae where they carry and integrate the diphtheria toxin gene between strains. Therefore, phage mediation results from transduction processes. \boxed{\text{Transduction}} ``` To ensure clarity while focusing only on essential details:

Phages cause genetic changes in \( Corynebacterium \) species like \( C. \) diphteriae through transduction, leading to the spread of virulence factors such as the diphtheria toxin gene. Thus, these modifications result from phage-mediated transduction.\(\boxed{\text{Transduction}}\) ```

This summary captures the core idea succinctly within two sentences focused on clinically significant aspects related to the mechanism described.
